# ClockBoard

> A React + Vite web app that shows the current time in five distinctive visual styles with integrated multi-timer and stopwatch support.

## What it does

ClockBoard renders a full-screen clock in one of five modes the user can switch between at any time:

- **Simple** (`/`) — clean digital clock
- **Flipboard** (`/flipboard`) — animated flip-board digits (split-flap display)
- **Digital** (`/digital`) — seven-segment LED-style display
- **Hourglass** (`/hourglass`) — animated sand falling in real time
- **Win95** (`/win95`) — Windows 95 retro desktop with draggable windows

Alongside the clock, users can create and run multiple countdown timers (with labels, presets, and an audio alert on completion) and stopwatches simultaneously.

## Tech stack

- React 18 + React Router 6
- Vite 5 (build tool / dev server)
- React Context API (state management)
- localStorage (persistence)
- Plain CSS with custom properties (theming)
- `@base-ui-components/react` for accessible primitives
- FontAwesome 5 for icons

## Source layout

```
src/
  App.jsx                  # Routes and top-level providers
  main.jsx                 # ReactDOM entry point
  components/
    Clock.jsx              # Digital clock face
    Nav.jsx                # Sidebar navigation + settings trigger
    FlipDigit.jsx          # Animated flip-card digit
    FlipLabel.jsx          # Animated flip-card label
    Timer.jsx              # Single countdown timer
    Stopwatch.jsx          # Single stopwatch
    TimersSection.jsx      # Container for all timers and stopwatches
  pages/
    ClockBoard.jsx         # Simple clock page
    Airport.jsx            # Flip-board page
    Digital.jsx            # Seven-segment page
    Hourglass.jsx          # Hourglass page
    Win95.jsx              # Win95 retro page
  context/
    AppContext.jsx         # Timers, stopwatches, fullscreen, UI state
    SettingsContext.jsx    # Theme, display toggles (seconds, AM/PM, date)
    ServerContext.jsx      # Optional backend sync + time drift correction
  data/
    teams.js               # 32 NFL team color palettes + custom theme slot
  styles/
    airport.css
    digital.css
    hourglass.css
    win95.css
assets/
  styles/styles.css        # Global styles + CSS variable definitions
  media/fonts/             # Endzone Sans, Ubuntu Mono, Droid Sans Mono
  media/timer.mp3          # Completion chime
```

## State management

Three React Context providers wrap the app:

| Context | Responsibility |
|---|---|
| `AppContext` | Active timers list, active stopwatches list, fullscreen flag, control-panel visibility |
| `SettingsContext` | Selected theme, alt-theme flag, showSeconds, showMeridiem, showDate, hourglassInterval, defaultRoute |
| `ServerContext` | Server URL, request headers, calculated time drift from `worldtimeapi.org` |

All context consumers use `useContext`; no prop drilling for global state.

## Persistence (localStorage keys)

| Key | Value |
|---|---|
| `cb_timers` | JSON array of timer objects |
| `cb_stopwatches` | JSON array of stopwatch objects |
| `cb_theme` | Theme name string |
| `cb_altTheme` | Boolean — swap primary/secondary colors |
| `cb_showSeconds` | Boolean |
| `cb_showMeridum` | Boolean |
| `cb_showDate` | Boolean |
| `cb_hourglassInterval` | `"hourly"` \| `"daily"` \| `"monthly"` \| `"yearly"` |
| `cb_defaultRoute` | Route path string |
| `cb_serverUrl` | Optional backend URL |
| `cb_serverHeaders` | JSON string of HTTP headers |

## Theming

Themes are applied by setting CSS custom properties on `:root`:
- `--primary` and `--secondary` — main brand colors
- Derived variables for backgrounds, text, borders, etc.

The `altTheme` flag swaps `--primary` and `--secondary`. Users can also create fully custom themes by picking arbitrary colors.

NFL team themes are defined in `src/data/teams.js` as `{ name, primary, secondary }` objects for all 32 teams.

## Notable implementation details

- **Time drift correction** — `ServerContext` fetches from `worldtimeapi.org`, measures round-trip latency, and computes offset to keep the displayed time accurate.
- **Gamepad support** — `GamepadService.js` is a singleton that polls the Gamepad API and dispatches custom events consumed by the app.
- **Flip animation** — `FlipDigit` uses CSS 3D transforms and a two-phase animation (top half flips down, bottom half reveals) to replicate a mechanical split-flap display.
- **Win95 z-ordering** — a module-level `let zTop = 100` counter increments whenever a window is focused, applied as inline `z-index`.
- **Auto-hiding controls** — the control panel fades out after 2 seconds of mouse inactivity using a `setTimeout` cleared on `mousemove`.

## Running locally

```bash
npm install
npm run dev       # http://localhost:5173
npm run build     # outputs to dist/
npm run preview   # serve the dist/ build
```
